title: Monte Carlo Option Pricing
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Monte Carlo Option Pricing: Monte Carlo option pricing: estimate exotic option price via simulation with variance reduction techniques. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (S.mc.path_generation…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~10; discretization_error_Euler_Milstein, random_seed_variance set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: C = e^{-rT} * E[max(S_T - K, 0)]; estimate by sample average over N_paths simulated paths
E.world_state_x: option price C and Greeks (delta, gamma, vega)
E.observation_y: simulated payoffs g(S_T ** i) for i=1..N_paths

W.regime: Existence of the recovered option_price_scalar is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 10); discretization_error_Euler_Milstein dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: standard error scales as 1/sqrt(N_paths); quasi-MC as O(log ** d N / N). Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~10. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |discretization_error_Euler_Milstein|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_paths * N_timesteps) for path generation per iteration
